config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan, di mana masyarakat kuat justru karena hak tiap orang dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily that idea can turn into excuse-making for government overreach and abuse. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be limited by constitutional rights, or else the rights become meaningless whenever they are inconvenient. To me, the burden should be on society to justify any restriction on an individual, not the other way around. A healthy society protects people first and then asks them to contribute, rather than treating them as tools for collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengorbankan kelompok yang lemah, padahal justru di situlah hak individu paling perlu dilindungi. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh diganggu gugat sembarangan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang bisa menjaga keseimbangan tanpa menjadikan orang sebagai alat demi tujuan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that principle has too often been used to justify overreach, censorship, and discrimination. That said, I do not buy an absolute individualism either, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require real limits on personal freedom. The key difference is that those limits should be narrow, transparent, and legally constrained, not granted just because a majority says it is for the common good. From my perspective, a society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and asks for collective sacrifice only when it is truly necessary and carefully justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan kelompok kecil, atau membiarkan ketidakadilan, jadi saya tidak percaya itu aman kalau dijadikan prinsip utama. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang butuh pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terlihat dari kemampuannya melindungi individu paling rentan, bukan dari seberapa mudah ia meminta orang berkorban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has repeatedly been used to excuse government overreach and discrimination. I do think society can sometimes impose narrow limits for public health, safety, or basic order, but those limits need to be tightly justified and legally constrained rather than treated as a blank check. When people say "the common good" without clear limits, it often ends up meaning the powerful get to decide whose rights are expendable. To me, a free society protects individual rights first and only asks for collective sacrifice when it is genuinely necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
